coreCompetencies: Basic understanding of embedded systems and microcontrollers

Elementary use of CoAP and HTTP for constrained devices
Connecting devices to an IoT cloud platform
Basic understanding of edge versus cloud processing
Documenting simple device-to-cloud architectures
Elementary notions of hardware, GPIO and serial buses

coreCompetencies: Developing firmware in C or C++ for microcontrollers
Building IoT gateways that bridge devices to the cloud
Working across the protocol stack (MQTT, CoAP, TLS)
Edge processing and local filtering of telemetry
Power management and low-power sleep modes
Basic sensor fusion from multiple inputs
Device security: secure boot, keys and certificates
Ingesting device data into cloud services
complementaryCompetencies: Development on an RTOS such as FreeRTOS or Zephyr
Long-range connectivity with LoRaWAN or NB-IoT

coreCompetencies: Designing end-to-end IoT architectures from device to cloud

IoT orchestration and device-lifecycle management
Establishing device security and provisioning standards

indicators: Is a reference in edge computing
